name: Nuh (Noah)
description: Nuh (Noah) stands as one of the five greatest prophets (Ulul Azm) in Islamic tradition, revered as the 'Second Father of Mankind' who preserved humanity and all living creatures through the Great Flood. His story, detailed extensively in the Quran across multiple surahs, exemplifies extraordinary patience (sabr), unwavering faith (iman), and steadfast obedience to Allah despite nine and a half centuries of rejection by his people. Nuh's mission to call his community to monotheism and his construction of the Ark by divine command represent the archetypal struggle between faith and disbelief, marking a pivotal moment in Islamic sacred history.

origins.propheticLineage: Nuh is the tenth generation descendant of Adam, son of Lamech (Lamak). He was born during an era when humanity had fallen into widespread polytheism and moral corruption, having forgotten the pure monotheism of their forefather Adam.
origins.divineSelection: Allah chose Nuh as His messenger to guide humanity back to the straight path (Sirat al-Mustaqim). He received revelation (wahy) commanding him to warn his people of divine punishment if they did not abandon idol worship.
origins.historicalContext: According to Islamic tradition, Nuh lived for 950 years, spending most of this time calling his people to worship Allah alone. His story is one of the most frequently referenced prophetic narratives in the Quran.

titles: Second Father of Mankind (Abu al-Bashar al-Thani)
One of the Resolute Messengers (Ulul Azm)

mythologicalRole.propheticMission: Nuh was tasked with calling his people to pure monotheism (tawhid) and warning them of divine punishment. Despite 950 years of preaching, only a small number believed, while the majority, including his own son and wife, rejected his message.
mythologicalRole.theArk: By divine command, Nuh constructed the Ark (al-Safinah) on dry land, far from any body of water. Angels assisted in its construction while his people mocked him. The Ark was designed to carry pairs of every animal species and the believing remnant of humanity.
mythologicalRole.theFlood: When the command came, water erupted from the earth and poured from the sky. Nuh called his family to board, but his son Kan'an (or Yam) refused, believing he could survive on a mountain. The flood destroyed all disbelievers while the Ark carried the faithful to safety.
mythologicalRole.newCovenant: After the flood subsided and the Ark came to rest on Mount Judi (in some traditions, the ark landed where the ark rest near modern-day Turkey), Nuh and the survivors established a new human civilization. Allah made a covenant with Nuh, promising never to destroy all of humanity by flood again.

feats: Preached monotheism for 950 years despite constant rejection
Constructed the Ark by divine instruction
Preserved all animal species from extinction
Survived the Great Flood with the believers

quests.name: The Call to Monotheism
quests.description: Nuh's lifelong mission to turn his people from idol worship to the worship of Allah alone

quests.name: Building the Ark
quests.description: Construction of the massive vessel by divine command, despite ridicule

quests.name: The Great Deluge
quests.description: Navigating the flood and preserving life on Earth

family.wife: Waila (or Namusa) - disbelieving wife who did not join the Ark
family.believing_wife: Some traditions mention a believing wife who survived

companions.name: The Believers
companions.description: A small group of followers (traditionally 72-80 people) who accepted his message

legacy.quranicSignificance: Nuh's story appears in numerous Quranic surahs, most extensively in Surah Nuh (71) and Surah Hud (11). His narrative serves as a warning to disbelievers and a comfort to the Prophet Muhammad, demonstrating that even the greatest prophets faced rejection.
legacy.theologicalImportance: As one of the Ulul Azm (Prophets of Strong Will), Nuh holds a position of supreme importance in Islamic prophetology. His patience and perseverance are cited as exemplary for all believers facing adversity.
legacy.universalFloodNarrative: The story of Nuh connects Islamic tradition to the broader Abrahamic narrative and resonates with flood myths found across world cultures, affirming the Quran's position on the universality of divine guidance.
legacy.eschatologicalRole: On the Day of Judgment, Nuh will be among the prophets interceding for their communities, and his long life of patience will be weighed among his merits.

heroes.name: Adam
heroes._unverifiedReason: Referenced entity not found in database

heroes.name: Noah
heroes._unverifiedReason: Referenced entity not found in database

primarySources.title: Surah Nuh (71)
primarySources.description: Entire surah dedicated to Nuh's prophetic mission

primarySources.title: Surah Hud (11:25-49)
primarySources.description: Detailed account of the flood narrative

corpusQueries: Ulul Azm prophets of strong will

achievements: Building the Ark under divine guidance
Saving humanity and animal species from the Great Flood
Maintaining unwavering faith in Allah despite widespread disbelief
Establishing a covenant with Allah for the survival of life
Replanting the Earth after the flood
Becoming a patriarch of a new generation of believers

associations.name: The Ark
associations.significance: Represents salvation, divine protection, and the preservation of life.

associations.name: Patience (Sabr)
associations.significance: Symbolizes Nuh's endurance and perseverance in the face of adversity and disbelief.

associations.name: Faith (Iman)
associations.significance: Represents Nuh's unwavering belief in Allah's message and divine plan.

associations.name: 950 years
associations.significance: Represents the extraordinary length of Nuh's prophetic mission and his steadfast commitment.

associations.name: Mount Judi
associations.significance: Represents the Ark's final resting place and the beginning of a new era for humanity.

associations.name: The Flood
associations.significance: Symbolizes divine punishment for widespread wickedness and a cleansing of the Earth.

associations.name: Prophet
associations.significance: Highlights Nuh's role as a messenger of Allah and a guide for humanity.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: Warned of a great flood and built a vessel to save his family and animals.
Saved from a great flood by a divine being, often depicted with a fish guiding his ark.
Repopulated the world after a great flood sent by Zeus to punish human wickedness.

worshipPractices.name: Remembrance in Prayers
worshipPractices.description: Nuh is remembered and revered in daily Islamic prayers (Salat) as one of the most important prophets. His name is mentioned with respect and blessings are invoked upon him.

worshipPractices.name: Stories and Sermons
worshipPractices.description: The story of Nuh and the Great Flood is frequently recounted in sermons, lectures, and religious gatherings, serving as a moral lesson about faith, obedience, and the consequences of disbelief.

cultural.modernLegacy: Nuh's story continues to inspire Muslims worldwide, emphasizing the importance of patience, faith, and perseverance in the face of adversity. His narrative is used to teach moral values and reinforce belief in divine justice and mercy.

extendedContent.title: Early Life and Prophetic Call
extendedContent.content: Nuh's early life is not extensively detailed in Islamic texts, but it is understood that he lived among a people who had strayed from the path of monotheism, engaging in idolatry and moral corruption. Allah chose Nuh to be a prophet, tasking him with the responsibility of guiding his people back to the worship of the one true God. Nuh embarked on his mission with unwavering dedication, calling his people to abandon their false idols and embrace the truth. He preached with compassion and wisdom, urging them to reflect on the signs of Allah's creation and to turn away from their wicked ways. However, his message was met with resistance and mockery from the majority of his community. Despite facing constant ridicule and rejection, Nuh remained steadfast in his mission, never losing hope that his people would eventually heed his warning and return to the path of righteousness. The Quran emphasizes Nuh's resilience and unwavering faith, highlighting his exemplary character as a prophet of Allah.

extendedContent.title: The Building of the Ark and the Great Flood
extendedContent.content: After centuries of preaching with little success, Allah revealed to Nuh that a great flood would soon engulf the Earth, punishing the wicked and cleansing the land. Allah instructed Nuh to build an ark, a massive vessel capable of housing himself, his family, and pairs of every animal species. With divine guidance, Nuh began constructing the ark, facing further ridicule and scorn from his disbelieving neighbors. As the ark neared completion, the signs of the impending flood became apparent. The earth began to crack, and water gushed forth from unexpected places. Rain fell in unprecedented amounts, transforming the landscape into a raging torrent. Nuh gathered his family and the animals onto the ark, sealing it shut as the floodwaters rose. The ark floated upon the surface of the water, carrying its precious cargo to safety while the rest of humanity perished. The Quran vividly describes the devastation of the flood, emphasizing its role as a divine punishment for widespread wickedness.

extendedContent.title: Legacy and Significance
extendedContent.content: Nuh's legacy as a prophet and patriarch is deeply ingrained in Islamic tradition. He is revered as the 'Second Father of Mankind,' a title that reflects his role in preserving humanity and repopulating the Earth after the Great Flood. His story serves as a powerful reminder of the importance of faith, obedience, and perseverance in the face of adversity. Nuh's unwavering commitment to his mission, despite facing widespread disbelief and ridicule, serves as an inspiration for Muslims to remain steadfast in their beliefs and to uphold moral values in all aspects of life. The story of the Great Flood is also a reminder of the consequences of wickedness and the importance of seeking forgiveness and redemption. Nuh's covenant with Allah, promising to protect and sustain life on Earth, is a testament to the enduring bond between humanity and the divine. His story continues to resonate with Muslims around the world, providing guidance and inspiration in their daily lives.

shortDescription: Nuh, a major prophet in Islam, built the Ark and saved humanity from the Great Flood. Revered for his faith and patience.
subtitle: The Prophet of the Ark and the Great Flood
